name: architect-implementer-reviewer
summary: 3-agent trio with explicit design gate: Architect drafts the plan, Implementer executes, Reviewer gates the merge.
when_to_use: Medium-to-large features with non-trivial design choices where the team wants design review before code is written.

file_ownership.implementer: writes src/ and tests/ after architect signs off the design doc
file_ownership.reviewer: writes nothing; posts review comments to .claude/reviews/

coordination: 1. Architect creates docs/design/{feature}.md with constraints, invariants, and acceptance criteria.
2. Implementer waits for architect file to exist; reads it; implements; writes tests.
3. Reviewer runs after implementer; produces review with [BLOCK]/[NIT]/[OK] verdicts.
4. Loop 2-3 until reviewer returns [OK] across all required checks.
worktree_guidance: Single worktree. Roles serialize — not parallel.
cost_estimate: ~$3-5 per feature. Architect pass ~$1, Implementer pass ~$1-2, Reviewer pass ~$1-2.
anti_patterns: Do not run architect and implementer in parallel — implementer will drift from the as-yet-unwritten design.
Reviewer must be a separate agent (not the architect) to catch blind spots in the original design.
